features.summary: Deere reported strong Q1 2021 results with net sales up 19% to $9.1 billion and net income of $1.224 billion ($3.87 per share). Equipment Operations achieved a 17% margin, the highest in company history. The company implemented new segment reporting, splitting Agriculture & Turf into Production & Precision Agriculture and Small Agriculture & Turf segments. Both segments performed well with 21% and 18.6% operating margins respectively. Construction & Forestry also showed improvement with 21% sales growth.
features.management_outlook: Management raised full-year net income guidance to $4.6-5.0 billion, reflecting strong market fundamentals and order book strength. Large tractor order books extend into Q4, and early order programs showed double-digit increases. The company expects Production & Precision Ag margins of 19.5-20.5% and Small Ag & Turf margins of 14.5-15.5% for fiscal 2021. Construction & Forestry is forecast to achieve 10.5-11.5% margins with industry sales expected to grow 5-10%.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints remain a significant challenge with tight component availability requiring week-to-week management of some parts. The company faces $500 million in additional material and freight cost headwinds, primarily affecting Production & Precision Ag. COVID-related absenteeism and electronic component shortages continue to impact operations. Management acknowledges the supply situation is very fluid and will remain challenging throughout the foreseeable future.
features.key_drivers: Strong agricultural fundamentals drove demand with higher commodity prices and improved farmer sentiment reflected in robust order books. Precision agriculture technology adoption continues accelerating, contributing to higher average selling prices and margin expansion. Price realization was exceptionally strong at nearly 8 points for Production & Precision Ag, driven by mid-year adjustments, new product launches, and reduced incentive spending due to tight inventory levels. The company's sustainability initiatives and precision agriculture solutions deliver $40 per acre in customer value.
